rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical selenylation of alkenes (three-component carboselenylation with styrenes/arenes) and direct C-H selenylation of activated arenes via Se-Se bond activation of diselenides without external oxidants
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of diselenide (RSeSeR) generates a selenium-centered radical (RSe•) and a selenium anion (RSe-); the latter is reoxidized at the anode to RSe•. After radical addition to alkene/arene, the resulting carbon-centered radical is anodically oxidized to a cation, and H2 is evolved at the cathode (paired electrolysis).

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from PhSe• addition to styrene (intermediate B)
benzylic carbocation after anodic oxidation of B
arenium/thiophenyl radical adduct from PhSe• addition to 2-ethylthiophene (intermediate C)

electron_flow: Cathodic reduction cleaves the Se-Se bond of PhSeSePh into PhSe• and PhSe- (the anion is reoxidized at the anode to PhSe•). PhSe• adds to a styrene to give a benzylic radical, which is anodically oxidized to a benzylic cation, trapped by a thiophene nucleophile, and deprotonated to product; on the arene branch PhSe• adds to the activated arene to give a radical that is anodically oxidized and deprotonated, with H2 evolution at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl diselenide (1a) - cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 1.64 V and reduction peak at -0.98 V observed, indicating both oxidation and reduction processes are accessible in the catalytic cycle

probe_reagents.name: styrene (2a) - cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 2.51 V, too high to be oxidized first under reaction conditions

probe_reagents.name: 2-ethylthiophene (3a) - cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at ~2.54 V, ruling out arene oxidation as the initiating event

probe_reagents.name: ITO electrode substitution
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing carbon anode with ITO gave no reaction; replacing Pt cathode with ITO decreased yield - indicates electrode plays vital role beyond simple electron transfer

probe_reagents.name: kinetic profile vs 3a concentration
probe_reagents.observation: Higher concentration of 3a (0.6 vs 0.3 mmol) increased rate/yield of 4a, consistent with arene trapping of cationic intermediate

probe_reagents.name: time-course of 4a vs 5b
probe_reagents.observation: Three-component product 4a accumulates while diselenylated arene byproduct 5b stays near 0%, indicating alkene pathway dominates when styrene present

notes: Authors cannot rule out an alternative initiation in which PhSeSePh is first anodically oxidized to a radical cation, fragmenting into PhSe• and PhSe+; the favored pathway is cathodic reduction of the Se-Se bond followed by anodic reoxidation of PhSe-. Two parallel product channels: radical-polar crossover with styrene (route a, giving aryl-alkyl selenoethers 4) and radical addition/oxidation/deprotonation on activated arenes (route b, giving aryl-aryl selenoethers 5). H2 evolution at the cathode balances the anodic oxidation events (paired electrolysis).
